news.title: From Popp to Diani: 10 women’s transfers you may have missed during the men’s World Cup
news.lede: Sam Kerr’s and Alexia Putellas’s moves were hard to miss but these transfers may have escaped your attention. There were a host of big women’s football transfers which were hard to miss across June and July, despite the dominance of the men’s World Cup. Among them were Beth Mead’s move from Arsenal to Manchester City, Mary Earps and Georgia Stanway returning to the Women’s Super League with London City Lionesses and Arsenal, Sam Kerr leaving Chelsea to return to Gotham FC and, most prominently, Alexia Putellas’s game-changing switch from Barcelona to London City. But here are 10 transfers you might have missed while attention was focused on the action in the US, Canada and Mexico.

news.title: Bayern Munich star Michael Olise plays in NYC pickup game
news.lede: You have to give it to Bayern Munich and France superstar Michael Olise, the man does love football. Well, after France lost the third-place game at the World Cup to England, the 24-year-old could not stay away from the game for long, so he joined a pickup game in New York City. Imagine you are stretching trying to get loose after sitting in a cube all day pouring through spreadsheets and you look across the field and have to defend Olise. Not just Olise, though, but Olise wearing some wild pants.

news.title: WATCH: Spanish Formula E star talks football after World Cup triumph
news.lede: Cupra Kiro's Pepe Marti told Motorsport Week which member of Spain's victorious World Cup team he most like to take for a hot lap, ahead of this weekend's Formula E Tokyo E-Prix . The Japanese capital plays host to the all-electric championship for the third time, but for the first time, will take place under the city's glittering lights as a night race. Such an event has boosted interest in the race and the championship, and has contributed to the city being given a spot on the calendar for a fourth season in a row, in what will be the first campaign of the upcoming Gen4 era. Marti has excelled in his debut season with the American, Silverstone-based squad, with two podiums, taking him to a current haul of 58 points, 30 more than teammate Dan Ticktum.

news.title: Norway considers filing Fifa ethics complaint over Trump role in World Cup red card saga
news.lede: Norwegian federation president: Fifa on a ‘slippery slope’. Trump called Infantino for review of Balogun’s red. Donald Trump’s role in clearing Folarin Balogun from a mandatory one-game ban at the World Cup is set to provoke a formal complaint to Fifa ethics investigators. The Norway soccer federation said Thursday its board of directors will consider filing the complaint at its next meeting, likely on 6 August.

news.title: The president of the Argentine Football Association breaks his silence: do not be fooled by "conspiracy theories"
news.lede: Tapia admits that Spain deserved the 2026 World Cup title. The 2026 World Cup final won't be forgotten in a hurry. Not just for the joy that swept across Spain after the coronation, but for the wave of controversy and the hundreds of conspiracy theories that erupted in Argentina after Ferran Torres's goal stripped the "tango dancers" of their world crown. Explanations for the defeat have spread like wildfire in Argentina over the past hours.

news.title: North Africa's Football Rise: World Cup Dreams Ahead!
news.lede: North African football is currently enjoying a significant upswing. As of early 2025, Egypt, Algeria, and Tunisia were all ranked within the top 50 in the FIFA world rankings, with Morocco comfortably positioned within the top 15 for three consecutive years. The forthcoming World Cup in Casablanca 2030 shines a spotlight on Morocco, presenting a potential opportunity for the Atlas Lions to claim football's most coveted trophy. Morocco's impressive performance in the 2022 World Cup solidified its position in football history.

news.title: England World Cup ratings: Every player ranked from first to worst
news.lede: This content is provided by Exco Player and may use cookies or similar technologies. Please click 'Allow and Continue' below to load the content. While England’s World Cup ended in disappointment, third place marked their best finish since 1966. The Three Lions secured the bronze medal with a 6-4 win over France in Miami, Florida, having been beaten 2-1 by Argentina in Atlanta, Georgia.

news.title: Spain's World Cup triumph ends with players receiving their body weight in tomatoes
news.lede: Spain's World Cup triumph has brought medals, global recognition and record prize money, but for Fabián Ruiz and Gavi, one of the most memorable rewards arrived hundreds of miles from New York New Jersey Stadium. Football News: After winning the 2026 FIFA World Cup, Spain's Fabián Ruiz and Gavi received a unique hometown honor: their body weight in tomatoes during a civic celebration in Los Palacios y Villafranca. This tradition marks a quirky yet heartfelt tribute to local football heroes following their historic triumph.
